2002-05-01.title: 4 Militants Die in Clashes With Allies Near Pakistan
2002-05-01.abstract: Military officials say force of fewer than 1,000 allied troops under United States command sweeping Afghanistan's border with Pakistan has killed four suspected Taliban or Al Qaeda fighters in past two days; say force, working in and out of border area for over week in bid to keep fighters who escaped earlier into Pakistan from regrouping in Afghanistan, has found large arms caches, but few adversaries; force includes American, Australian, British and Afghan fighters; Pakistani intelligence official says major US-led military operation will probably begin in area around Khost in next day (M)

2002-05-01.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: Farrakhan Barred
2002-05-01.abstract: British appeals court reverses lower court ruling and upholds government's decision to bar Nation of Islam leader Louis Farrakhan from entering nation; cites his 'notorious opinions' and uneasy state of race and religious relations in Britain; he weighs appeal (S)

2002-05-01.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: London Synagogue Attacked
2002-05-01.abstract: Vandals ransack London synagogue; attack startles nation that has been largely immune to anti-Jewish attacks (S)

2002-05-01.title: Hindu Party Wins in Critical Vote
2002-05-01.abstract: India's Hindu nationalist-led government, after marathon debate in which it is harshly criticized by opponents and some allies, defeats motion to criticize it for failing to control Hindu mobs who have killed hundreds of Muslims in Gujarat state over past two months; motion is defeated, 276-182; Min George Fernandes holds violence is no different than sectarian riots that occured under Congress Party rule; Congress Party head Sonia Gandhi, often reticent, has forcefully criticized government on issue (S)

2002-05-01.title: U.N. May Drop Inquiry at Jenin As Israel Resists
2002-05-01.abstract: Israel again blocks proposed fact-finding mission to probe Apr fighting at Jenin refugee camp, spurring United Nations Sec Gen Kofi Annan to weigh disbanding investigative team; Israeli officials say they fear biased probe that could possibly expose army to war-crimes trial; Prime Min Ariel Sharon invokes own service as soldier in declaring he will protect troops; holds Israel made extraordinary efforts not to harm innocent people; Palestinian officials charge cover-up; United States, in shift away from its aggressive backing of probe, says it can do nothing if Annan drops probe; US and British officials meet, Ramallah, to work out details for moving six wanted men from Yasir Arafat's beseiged compound into Palestinian prison under international guardianship; Israel withdraws its ground forces from Hebron (M)

2002-05-02.title: World Briefing | Americas: Mexico: Freedom Of Information
2002-05-02.abstract: Mexican Senate unanimously approves nation's first freedom of information law, requiring government to open records and files to public; law is seen as important step toward ending seven decades of government corruption and abuses shrouded by secrecy (S)

2002-05-02.title: In Afghan War, Top Exiles Printed Themselves a Fortune
2002-05-02.abstract: Senior government officials in Afghanistan suspect that members of former Northern Alliance, which did most of fighting against Taliban, are unloading huge stores of bank notes they printed for themselves during their years in the wilderness; say when warlords of old Northern Alliance cash in their wads of cash for dollars, they get wealthy and country's currency, afghani, takes further plunge; recent collapse of Afghanistan's currency, and skulduggery surrounding it, stands as measure of weakness of country's fledgling government, which is unable to control even supply of its own money; Anwar Ahadi, governor general of central bank, points to murky relationship between what was Northern Alliance and Russian-Swiss company that for years held exclusive rights to print Afghan money; photo (M)

2002-05-02.title: Indonesian Official Urges U.S. to Resume Military Assistance
2002-05-02.abstract: Laksamana Sukardi, close adviser to Pres Megawati Sukarnoputri of Indonesia, says United States should drop restrictions imposed three years ago on assistance to Indonesian military and help restore 'tarnished reputation' of his nation's army; since Sept 11 Washington has repeatedly pressed Indonesia to take firmer action against groups and individuals suspected of having ties to Osama bin Laden and his Al Qaeda terrorism network; Sukardi says this is impossible task if military remains discredited in eyes of public; Indonesian Government wants to resume military training in US for its officers; Sukardi stops short of advocating that US help supply military with weapons (M)

2002-05-02.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Editor Shot To Death
2002-05-02.abstract: Valery Ivanov, newspaper editor in Togliatti, Russia, is killed after publishing articles on corruption in local government and on ties between local criminal groups and auto producer Avtovaz, which is based in city; Ivanov was also general director of local television station whose previous two directors were killed (S)

2002-05-02.title: U.S. Drops Last Link of Iraq to 9/11
2002-05-02.abstract: Senior Bush administration official says FBI and CIA analysts have concluded there is no evidence that Mohamed Atta, suspected ringleader of Sept 11 attacks, met with Iraqi intelligence officer before hijackings; reports of meeting came from Czech officials like Prime Min Milos Zeman (S)

2002-05-03.title: India Jet Hits Building and Kills 7
2002-05-03.abstract: A Indian MIG-21 fighter jet caught fire today and crashed into a three story bank building in the northwestern city of Jalandhar. Seven people were killed and 14 wounded.

2002-05-03.title: World Briefing: Americas
2002-05-03.abstract: Americas MEXICO: More Chances For Women; BRAZIL: Cloners Didn't Use Sonogram.

2002-05-03.title: Ida Milgrom, 94, Dies; Helped Free a Son Held by Soviets
2002-05-03.abstract: Ida Milgrom, teacher, economist and musician who helped to lead international campaign to free her son, Soviet dissident Anatoly B. Shcharansky, from prison, dies at age 94; photo (M)

2002-05-03.title: Moscow Journal; Where 'Living While Black' Means Living in Fear
2002-05-03.abstract: Racial attacks have been on rise in past five years against Moscow's ethnic minorities, in particular Africans, Asians and people from Caucasus; problem is slowly gaining national attention, prompting Pres Vladimir V Putin, in address to nation last month, to warn against rise of fascist groups; attacks often appear to be organized; ambassadors from 37 African countries have signed highly unusual joint appeal to Foreign Ministry asking for more protection after spate of attacks on their families and embassy workers; attackers are young, between ages of 14 and 20, and often those who feel cast adrift in economic collapse and social upheaval that followed fall of Soviet Union; like skinhead groups across Europe, these teenagers turn their anger on people of color; photos (M)

2002-05-03.title: 'No' Votes And Nonvoters
2002-05-03.abstract: List of legislators who voted 'No' or did not vote on resolutions of support for Israel in House and Senate (S)

2002-05-04.title: 3 Killed in Raid in Nablus; Peace Conference Idea Wins Initial Support on Both Sides
2002-05-04.abstract: Israel and Palestinians express cautious support for peace conference announced by Bush administration, as Israeli soldiers launch raid into center of Nablus in which Israeli officer and two Palestinians are killed; in Bethlehem, Palestinian and Israeli negotiators remain deadlocked over siege at Church of the Nativity; much about conference remains unresolved, including its goals and topics of discussion; officials in Washington emphasize that it would be meeting of foreign ministers, not heads of state, in plan that seems intended to sidestep Prime Min Ariel Sharon's refusal to negotiate with Yasir Arafat because of Israel's belief that he sponsors terrorism; photos (M)

2002-05-04.title: Barbara Castle, British Radical, Is Dead at 91
2002-05-04.abstract: Barbara Anne Castle, former British Labor cabinet minister and determined socialist, dies at age of 91; photo (M)

2002-05-04.title: Kosovo Leader Confronts Old Foe, Milosevic, at War Crimes Tribunal
2002-05-04.abstract: Kosovo's leader, Ibrahim Rugova, testifies at war crimes trial of Slobodan Milosevic, who as leader of Serbia had Rugova arrested; Rugova, who had led campaign of peaceful resistance against Milosevic's rule in Kosovo, charges that serious war crimes were committed in Kosovo under Milosevic's leadership; Milosevic turns loud and aggressive as he cross-examines Rugova (M)

2002-05-04.title: U.S.-Russia Talks Inch Nuclear Deal Closer
2002-05-04.abstract: Sec of State Colin L Powell and Foreign Min Igor S Ivanov of Russia say they made substantial headway toward negotiating agreement on reducing nuclear weapons that could be signed by Pres Bush and Pres Putin at summit meeting in Russia on May 23; Powell and Ivanov acknowledge that differences remain, but tenor of their remarks leaves little doubt that there is growing optimism on both sides that pact could be hammered out in time for summit meeting (M)

2002-05-04.title: Mocked in Europe of Old, African Is Embraced at Home at Last
2002-05-04.abstract: Memorial service is held in Cape Town, South Africa, for Saartjie Baartman, young African woman who was lured to Europe in early 19th century with false promises of fame and fortune, but who died in Paris in 1816, penniless and friendless among people who derided her as circus freak; white scientists intent on proving inferiority of blacks dissected her and displayed her body parts in French museum; after years of wrangling between South Africa and France, her remains are removed from Musee de l'Homme in Paris and flown home; in South Africa, museum officials hope to arrange proper burials for skeletons of other Africans who were defiled in death in name of science; photos (M)

2002-05-05.title: Bangladesh Ferry Sinks in Storm And Scores Are Feared Drowned
2002-05-05.abstract: As many as 150 people may have drowned in sinking of double-decker ferry on Meghna River about 35 miles southeast of Dhaka, Bangladesh; 60 passengers are rescued; officials say 300 people may have been on board; photo (M)

2002-05-05.title: Yevgeny Svetlanov, Gifted Russian Conductor, Dies at 73
2002-05-05.abstract: Yevgeny Svetlanov, Russian conductor, dies at age 73; photo (M)

2002-05-05.title: U.S. Is Pressing Hard for End to Church Siege
2002-05-05.abstract: Intense American pressure to resolve monthlong standoff between Israelis and Palestinians at Church of the Nativity in Bethlehem causes breakthrough in new talks involving top aides to Yasir Arafat and American and European diplomats; breakthrough follows Arafat's order to Palestinians to give list of those hiding in church to American and European Union diplomats; Israeli officials say 25 wanted men and about 140 civilians are inside complex; have demanded list for weeks; photo (M)

2002-05-05.title: On Eve of Vote, French Papers Rally Voters Against Rightist
2002-05-05.abstract: Left- and right-leaning French newspapers are virtually unanimous in running front-page editorials urging voters to reject far-right candidate, Jean-Marie Le Pen, and to give Pres Jacques Chirac landslide victory in May 5 runoff election for President (M)

2002-05-05.title: Senior Pentagon Official Urges Links With Moderate Muslims
2002-05-05.abstract: Deputy Defense Sec Paul D Wolfowitz warns of dangerous gap between West and Islam, speech, World Affairs Council, Monterrey, Calif; calls for broad American initiative to strengthen ties with moderate Muslim nations and with 'tolerant' people of Muslim world (S)

2002-05-06.title: U.S. Says It Will Not Support International Criminal Court
2002-05-06.abstract: Bush administration officials said today that the court's prosecutors will not be given any information to help them bring cases against any individuals.

2002-05-06.title: Israelis Release More Documents Accusing Arafat of Terror
2002-05-06.abstract: Israeli officials intensify campaign to discredit Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat, distributing three compilations of captured Palestinian documents to American officials in Jerusalem that Israelis contend are indisputable proof that he personally approved terror attacks; documents indicate Arafat approved payments to members of Fatah party who later carried out terror attacks, that his security service was aware that some Fatah members were engaging in terrorism and that militant groups Hamas and Islamic Jihad infiltrated parts of Palestinian security apparatus; do not appear to show definitively that Arafat ordered attacks (M)

2002-05-06.title: Sir Peter Shepheard, Architect And Landscape Designer, 88
2002-05-06.abstract: Sir Peter Shepeard, British architect, planner and landscape designer, dies at age 88; photos (M)

2002-05-06.title: Nepal Says Over 400 Rebels Are Dead After Several Battles
2002-05-06.abstract: Nepalese security forces report killing more than 400 Maoist rebels since May 2 in training camps hidden in remote areas, with deaths of only three soldiers; rout of rebels would be worst military blow yet to rebels in their six-year-long insurgency; rebel leader Pushpa Kamal Dahal calls for renewed peace negotiations; Prime Min Sher Bahadur Deuba, who engaged in three rounds of peace talks with Maoists in 2001, rejects overture; says government will not speak with rebels until they lay down arms and surrender (M)

2002-05-06.title: Korean Leader Quits His Party Over Scandal
2002-05-06.abstract: Pres Kim Dae Jung of South Korea resigns from Millennium Democratic Party in bid to insulate it from corruption allegations surrounding his children and several close aides; resignation follows arrest Kwon Ro Gap, close associate, on charges of bribery and stock manipulation; two of Kim's three sons also face summonses by prosecutors; are suspected of peddling influence in return for bribes (S)

2002-05-07.title: Washington Accuses Cuba Of Germ-Warfare Research
2002-05-07.abstract: Bush administration accuses Cuba of producing small quantities of germs that can be used in biological warfare and says Libya and Syria are also violating international treaties by making unconventional weapons; Under Sec of State John R Bolton, in speech to Heritage Foundation, publicly alludes to conclusions by American intelligence agencies in recent months; Cuban Embassy spokesmen Luis Fernandez denies assertions; accuses administration of trying to extend concern over international terrorism to Cuba to justify hard-line policies at time when US farmers and allies that view Cuba as natural market are advocating loosening of four-decade trade embargo (M)

2002-05-07.title: Man in the News; A Leader to Lean On; Jean-Pierre Raffarin
2002-05-07.abstract: Man-in-the News biographical portrait of Jean-Pierre Raffarin, selected by Pres Jacques Chirac as France's new premier; photo (M)

2002-05-07.title: Rightist Candidate in Netherlands Is Slain, and the Nation Is Stunned
2002-05-07.abstract: Pim Fortuyn, maverick right-wing populist and leading candidate in upcoming national elections in Netherlands, is shot and killed by gunman in parking lot of radio station in town of Hilversun, where he had given interview; police arrest unidentified 33-year-old Dutch citizen, who reportedly confesses; murder deeply stuns nation that sees itself as peaceful and egalitarian; raises specter of violence stalking European politics; Prime Wim Kok condemns murder in deeply emotional speech to nation; calls it tragedy not only for nation, but for its democracy; Fortuyn's rise in Dutch politics discussed; former sociology professor wanted to become country's first gay prime minister; carried same strong anti-immigrant message that has propelled resurgent far right to political triumphs elsewhere in Europe; recently stunned Dutch political establishment by winning power in Rotterdam, country's second largest city; public opinion polls predicted he would win one-fifth of vote in May 15 elections, and that his group would have been crucial to formation of next government; map; photo (M)

2002-05-07.title: Chirac's Pick: A Premier Friendly to Small Business
2002-05-07.abstract: French Pres Jacques Chirac moves quickly to capitalize on landslide re-election victory over rightist Jean-Marie Le Pen; selects center-right Prime Minister Jean-Pierre Raffarin to restore his conservative base and lead his party into legislative elections in June following resignation of Socialist Prime Min Lionel Jospin; move is seen recognition of anger of French voters at Paris-based government elite; Raffarin, affable and little-known former junior minister, is not member of Chirac's party but low-profile senator and head of regional council in western France; he is believed capable of bringing small entrepreneurs who voted for Le Pen back into conservative fold; map; photo (M)

2002-05-07.title: Deal to End Church Siege Is Snagged on Angry Details
2002-05-07.abstract: Agreement to end siege of Church of the Nativity in Bethlehem by Israeli forces and release 100 people inside is snagged on angry details; negotiators are deadlocked over how many of Palestinian gunmen inside church will be sent abroad, most likely to Italy, or whether exile will be permanent or temporary; photo (M)

2002-05-08.title: Deep Fears Behind Australia's Immigration Policy
2002-05-08.abstract: Australians' will to preserve their way of life drives popularity of rigid refugee policy introduced last year; photos; map (M)

2002-05-08.title: World Briefing: Asia
2002-05-08.abstract: Asia MALAYSIA: A Muslim Leader Criticizes Muslims; North KOREA: Talks On Nuclear Program; BANGLADESH: Ferry Death Toll Passes 200; TAIWAN: Water Rationing For Capital.

2002-05-08.title: Clinton to Represent Bush at Timor Event
2002-05-08.abstract: Pres Bush asks Bill Clinton to lead US delegation at East Timor's independence celebrations, first time he has given former president role in administration (S)

2002-05-08.title: Allies Exhume 23 Bodies Thought to Be Qaeda Fighters
2002-05-08.abstract: Canadian troops and American criminal investigators in Afghanistan's Tora Bora mountains exhume 23 bodies they believe are remains of Al Qaeda fighters, perhaps Osama bin Laden security contingent killed in air strike Dec 15; DNA testing is done before reburial; bin Laden's remains are not found (M)

2002-05-08.title: In Rotterdam, Even a Politician's Foes Pay Respects
2002-05-08.abstract: Thousands of people, from stolid Dutch burghers to African students and Turkish girls, stand in line for hours in Rotterdam to sign condolence books for murdered political leader Pim Fortuyn; photo (M)

2002-05-09.title: Nepal Battle Intensifying After Attack On Rebels
2002-05-09.abstract: Warfare between army and Maoist guerrillas has intensified in last week in Maoist-controlled western Nepal, isolated, mountainous region; rebels, responding to army attack on major base hidden deep in forest, attack army and police post, Gam; Prime Min Sher Bahadur Deuba meets with State Sec Colin L Powell, Washington, seeking military aid; says $20 million proposed by Bush administration is inadequate (M)

2002-05-09.title: North Koreans Seek Asylum At Consulates In China
2002-05-09.abstract: Seven North Koreans try to barge into consulates in Shenyang, China, in bid for political asylum; two succeed in entering American Consulate and five are interecepted by Chinese guards at Japanese Consulate; 28 North Koreans have gained safe passage to South Korea by forcing their way into Western diplomatic compounds in Beijing in last two months, angering and frustrating Chinese Government; map (M)

2002-05-09.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: Irish Guerrillas Sentenced
2002-05-09.abstract: London court sentences Declan Rafferty, Fintan O'Farrell and Michael MacDonald, members of renegade Real IRA, to 30 years in jail for trying to buy arms in Slovakia for their program of terror aimed at bringing down Northern Ireland peace plan (S)

2002-05-09.title: Mexico Reopens Inquiry
2002-05-09.abstract: Mexico's federal election board has reopened probe into financing of Pres Vicente Fox's 2000 election campaign at request of two major opposition parties; opposition parties have charged, without hard evidence, that Fox illgeally got foreign contributions (S)

2002-05-09.title: U.S. Attack On Warlord Aims to Help Interim Leader
2002-05-09.abstract: Pentagon and Bush administration officials say Central Intelligence Agency used missile fired from unmanned surveillance drone in unsuccessful bid to kill Gulbuddin Hekmatyar, Hezb-i-Islami militant leader who has vowed to topple Hamid Karzai's interim government in Afghanistan; attack is new tactic in war and shows Bush's determination to support Karzai; American officials cite evidence of Hekmatyar's efforts to bring down Karzai and strike at American and allied troops; Brig Roger Lane, who leads British task force in Afghanistan, says war is all but won against Al Qaeda and Taliban; says offensive operations will be over in matter of weeks, not months (M)

2002-05-10.title: World Briefing | Asia: Nepal: Rebels' Cease-Fire Rejected
2002-05-10.abstract: Maoist rebel leader Pushpa Kamal Dahal proposes monthlong cease-fire in fighting in Nepal; Prime Min Sher Bahadur Deuba rejects plan, saying Maoists betrayed him last year when they broke off peace talks; Deuba is in United States seeking military aid to help crush insurgency (S)

2002-05-10.title: Taiwan's Leader Proposes a Delegation as an Overture to China
2002-05-10.abstract: Pres Chen Shui-bian of Taiwan makes unexpected peace overture to Beijing; proposes to send informal delegation from his ruling Democratic Progressive Party to China late this summer; Taiwan and China have had informal contacts for last 10 years, but Chen's party has tended to advocate more independence from China; as result it has had frostier relations with mainland than other Taiwanese political parties; Taipei and Beijing each officially advocate principle that there is one China encompassing both mainland and island of Taiwan, but large faction of Chen's party favors explicit announcement of independence from mainland; there is no immediate reaction from Beijing to Chen's proposal (M)

2002-05-10.title: World Briefing: Asia
2002-05-10.abstract: Asia NEPAL: Rebels' Cease-fire Rejected; South KOREA: Opposition Names Candidate.

2002-05-10.title: An Anti-American Boycott Is Growing in the Arab World
2002-05-10.abstract: American support for Israel is driving grass-roots effort to punish America by boycotting its consumer products throughout Arab world; word is being spread via Internet, mosque sermons, fliers and even mobile phone messages; purchases of American goods generated by 300 million Arabs form very small part of American exports, but long boycott could retard spread of franchises and other products; boycotts are largely effort of individuals and small groups without government involvement; they reflect growing sentiment that Arabs should distance themselves from United States, and they want their governments to do likewise; photos (M)

2002-05-10.title: A Bittersweet Homecoming
2002-05-10.abstract: Salah Ajarma's return home after 39 days in the Church of the Nativity in Bethlehem was a bittersweet affair.

2002-05-11.title: World Briefing | Asia: Vietnam: Too Much Democracy
2002-05-11.abstract: Nong Duc Manh, head of Vietnam's Communist Party, says says democracy may be getting out of hand; calls for curb on public protests at government buildings; says people should confine complaints to proper government channels; photo (S)

2002-05-11.title: World Briefing | Africa: Uganda: Political Restrictions Eased
2002-05-11.abstract: Uganda's Parliament approves bill easing curbs on political parties; some lawmakers walk out, holding changes do not do enough to end one-party rule; new law lets parties operate but bars them from holding meetings or canvassing for support outside capital (S)

2002-05-11.title: Paper Fights Subpoena Of Journalist By U.N. Court
2002-05-11.abstract: Washington Post challenges subpoena served by United Nations war crimes tribunal on former Post reporter Jonathan Randal to testify in case of Radoslav Brdjanin, Bosnian Serb charged with genocide, persecution and deportation of non-Serbs during Bosnian war; wants court to determine circumstances under which journalists must testify; holds journalists working in conflict zones should not be required to testify unless absolutely vital because it could hamper their work and endanger their lives (M)

2002-05-11.title: In Church of Nativity, the Refuse of a Siege
2002-05-11.abstract: Church of Nativity, Bethlehem, is dirty but apparently undamaged after withdrawal of over 130 Palestinian gunmen, civilians and clergy members following 39-day seige by Israeli army; Israeli troops leave Manger Square; area residents, freed from nearly constant curfew, come to gape at church; some from area's dwindling Christian population come to pray and rejoice in tumult that breaks jealously guarded protocols that divide church's administration between Orthodox and Roman Catholic denominations; photos (M)

2002-05-11.title: A Pakistani Dancer and Her Life Underground
2002-05-11.abstract: Profile of Sheema Kermani, Pakistani dancer whose classical art form is now largely banned in increasingly conservative Muslim Pakistan; she deplores pressure on women to hide their bodies; is one of few dancers who have not retreated into exile or retirement; photo (M)

2002-05-12.title: Administration Says Russia Is Preparing Nuclear Tests
2002-05-12.abstract: Bush administration officials reportedly brief Congress on what they describe as disturbing intelligence indicating that Russia is preparing to resume nuclear tests, even as Pres Bush prepares to meet with Pres Vladimir V Putin to discuss arms control later this month; among members of Congress who receive briefing, reaction ranges from alarm to skepticism; some wonder if report is tactic to help clear way for US to resume nuclear testing; others are alarmed enough to draft bill that would call for access to Russian nuclear sites and allow work on new generaiton of American nuclear warheads; Russian officials maintain that their nuclear weapons program remains within constraints of Comprehensive Test Ban Treaty signed by Pres Clinton in 1996 but never ratified by Senate; Pres Bush contends it does not adequately protect nation's security interests, although his administration continues to honor test moratorium (M)

2002-05-12.title: North Korean Migrants Pull U.S. Into a Diplomatic Mess
2002-05-12.abstract: Diplomat says eight North Koreans who tried to storm into two foreign consulates in Shenyang, China, are seeking asylum in United States, not in South Korea; this pulls United States into messy international debate; it also puts to test Pres Bush's Feb expression of 'great sympathy and empathy' for North Korean people; other North Koreans who have sought refuge and asylum at United States Embassy in Beijing wanted to go to South Korea, which has policy of accepting North Koreans; request for asylum in US pushes Americans to take explicit stand on politically loaded question: should hundreds of thousands of North Koreans illegally in China be given protected political refugee status under United Nations convenants; map (M)

2002-05-12.title: At Embassy in Pakistan, Home Is Next Objective
2002-05-12.abstract: Amb Wendy Chamberlin will leave United States Embassy in Pakistan after nine months to return home to family, saying 'I'm a mother first'; her children are not with her because State Dept says Pakistan is unsafe for all but essential embassy workers; workers throughout embassy, majority of them men, are making same decision as Chamberlin, advising State Dept that they want to leave their assignments early rather then endure continuous separation from spouses and children; photo (M)

2002-05-12.title: Reporter's Notebook; To Be Young and in Negotiations: A U.N. Education
2002-05-12.abstract: Reporter's Notebook vignettes from United Nations' first General Assembly Special Session on Children, during which children are encouraged to be seen and heard (M)

2002-05-12.title: Rebuffing Sharon, Party Repudiates Palestinian State
2002-05-12.abstract: The Likud Party voted tonight in favor of a resolution never to allow the creation of a Palestinian state, defying its leader, Prime Minister Ariel Sharon.

2002-05-13.title: Beijing Journal; Where Toilets Are the Pits, a Push for Porcelain
2002-05-13.abstract: China turns enthusiastic attention to public bathrooms, long-neglected aspect of life; is spending millions of dollars to upgrade public restrooms in campaign quietly begun after joining World Trade Organization in 2001; lavish renovations of public restrooms in Beijing with porcelain flush toilets and lockable stalls described; standard toilet in much of rest of country is open pit in concrete floor; photos (M)

2002-05-13.title: Palestinians Sow Confusion And Doubt On Bombing
2002-05-13.abstract: Palestinian Authority's intelligence service head Amin al-Hendi says recent suicide bombing in pool hall in Tel Aviv, Israel, does not appear to be work of Palestinian terrorists, but rather was conducted by Israeli criminals against illegal gambling club; Israel dismisses remarks as baseless and irresponsible; says bombing was conducted by Hamas, radical Islamic group (M)

2002-05-13.title: Castro Says Carter Can Inspect Biotechnology Centers
2002-05-13.abstract: Former Pres Jimmy Carter begins five-day visit to Cuba with promise from Pres Fidel Castro that he can inspect any of island's biotechnology research facilities; offer made at welcoming ceremony in Havana seeks to blunt State Department allegations that Cuba has developed limited capacity to make biological weapons and shares biotechnology with rogue nations; Castro says Carter is free to meet with whomever he wishes; Carter in welcoming address says he is visiting as friend of Cuban people and looks forward to meeting with Cubans from all walks of life; his arrival in Havana and tour of city described; photo (M)

2002-05-13.title: A Bombing In Russia: A Massacre Of Children
2002-05-13.abstract: Terrorist bombing of outdoor market in Kaspisk, capital of Russian republic of Dagestan, takes heavy toll on children; 17 children died out of 41 fatalities and 30 more children were wounded; authorities say bomb appears to be antipersonnel mine, detonated by remote control; say bombing may be related to guerrilla war in neighboring Chechnya; maps; photo (M)

2002-05-13.title: For Sierra Leone Ballot, Hope Trumps Despair
2002-05-13.abstract: Streets of Freetown, Sierra Leone, are scenes of lively campaign rallies in last days leading up to country's first elections since brutal civil war in 1999; underscores how much things have changed in West African nation, where only two years ago rebels outsmarted and humiliated UN peacekeeping force, leaving thousands dead on streets; question looms over election over whether country can survive without current umbrella of Western and UN aid; despite governing party's slogan that everything is plentiful, war destroyed nearly everything in country; maps; photo (M)

2002-05-14.title: Minister Says India Won't Attack Pakistan
2002-05-14.abstract: Interview with India's Defense Min George Fernandes, who says India has no plans to launch military attack against Pakistan over next few months even if severely provoked; rising tensions between both countries in recent months over issue of Kashmir noted (M)

2002-05-14.title: Mexico Now Appears to Be Developing the Politics of Stalemate
2002-05-14.abstract: Mexico inches toward politics of stalemate as Institutional Revolutionary Party, former governing party, has succeeded in blocking coalition government of Pres Vicente Fox at every opportunity in Congress; Fox is struggling to consolidate political power in capital and throughout Mexico (M)

2002-05-14.title: Carter and Powell Cast Doubt on Bioarms in Cuba
2002-05-14.abstract: Former Pres Jimmy Carter says Bush administration officials who briefed him before trip to Cuba never mentioned any concerns about Cuban involvement in developing biological weapons; tours Center for Genetic Engineering and Biotechnology in Havana with Pres Fidel Castro; comments come as Sec of State Colin L Powell is casting doubt on recent assertions by State Dept official John R Bolton that Cuba has developed limited offensive biological warfare research and development effort (S)

2002-05-14.title: The Words Of Bush On Treaty
2002-05-14.abstract: Text of remarks by Pres Bush on new nuclear weapons treaty with Russia (S)

2002-05-14.title: Treaty Offers Pentagon New Flexibility for New Set of Nuclear Priorities
2002-05-14.abstract: Provision of new nuclear arms treaty between US and Russia, to be signed by Pres Bush and Pres Vladimir Putin at Moscow summit, discussed; graphs (M)

2002-05-15.title: World Briefing | Europe: Kosovo: Convictions In Serbs' Killings
2002-05-15.abstract: Ethnic Albanian man is sentenced to 15 years in prison for killing elderly Serbian woman in Kosovo, second conviction in recent days in revenge crimes against Serbs in province (S)

2002-05-15.title: Video of Death Of Journalist Is Seen in Court In Pakistan
2002-05-15.abstract: Video of beheading of American journalist Daniel Pearl is reportedly shown in Pakistani court in closed trial of Ahmed Omar Sheikh and three others charged with his kidnapping and murder (M)

2002-05-15.title: MOVIE CRAZY : Cannes, Hollywood... Bollywood & the world
2002-05-15.abstract: CANNES—  The Cannes Festival at this flashy little beach resort is striving to revamp its legendary frosty image and stave off grumbles from foreign guests, and the Hollywood contingent — so desired, yet so feared.

2002-05-15.title: Vivendi walks tightrope with balance sheet
2002-05-15.abstract: PARIS:—  Bowing to pressure from credit ratings agencies to reduce debt and improve transparency, Vivendi Universal on Tuesday agreed to the early settlement of a financing arrangement with Deutsche Bank that could have exposed the media company to billions of dollars in off-balance sheet risks.

2002-05-15.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: Segregating Asylum Seekers
2002-05-15.abstract: Britain, in latest bid to deal with growing numbers of migrants in urban areas, plans to build three centers to house new arrivals whose asylum applications are being processed; community groups and advocacy groups oppose plans (S)

2002-05-16.title: World Briefing: Asia
2002-05-16.abstract: PAKISTAN: Crackdown In The Wild Northwest ; CAMBODIA: A Poke At U.N.

2002-05-16.title: Blair Says Merrie Olde England Indeed Needs Shiny New Euro
2002-05-16.abstract: Prime Min Tony Blair of Britain edges closer to outright endorsement of joining Europe's single currency; says it would be 'betrayal of our national interest' if Britons choose to remain apart from new money for political reasons alone; membership in 12-nation euro zone is one of most contentious issues facing Blair; he is seen as being far more supportive of adopting euro than Gordon Brown, chancellor of exchequer; Blair indicates that Britons might be called to pass judgment on euro membership sooner than expected (M)

2002-05-16.title: World Briefing | Asia: Cambodia: A Poke At U.N.
2002-05-16.abstract: Prime Min Hun Sen suggests United Nations pulled out of talks with Cambodia on setting up trial of former Khmer Rouge leaders because it wanted to avoid embarrassment; bases his gibe on historical reality that United Nations continued to recognize brutal Communist movement throughout its reign of terror and for decade after; photo (S)

2002-05-16.title: West Bank Damages Put at $361 Million
2002-05-16.abstract: Israel's recent military offensive in West Bank causes physical damage estimated at $361 million by international donors who are mobilizing to finance reconstruction; by comparison, first 15 months of Israeli-Palestinian fighting caused estimated $305 million in damage; damage assessment is carried out by World Bank, United Nations agencies, European Union, United States Agency for International Development and officials from various countries, in cooperation with Palestinian Authority (M)

2002-05-16.title: Japan and China Intensify Dispute Over Korean Asylum Seekers
2002-05-16.abstract: Diplomatic dispute between China and Japan escalates over case of North Korean family seeking political asylum in Japanese consulate last week; Chinese police arrested family, including 2-year-old girl, within Japanese compound in Shenyang, prompting angry protests, accusations and news conferences about who should control their fate; crisis threatens careers of several high-level Japanese officials, including foreign minister; dispute originates from disagreement over whether Chinese police should have been in Japanese consular compound (M)

2002-05-17.title: Taliban Killed in New Operation in Afghanistan Mountains
2002-05-17.abstract: Nearly 1,000 British commandos were airlifted to eastern Afghanistan today after an Australian patrol came under fire.

2002-05-17.title: World Briefing: Africa
2002-05-17.abstract: Africa ZIMBABWE: Black Squatters Face Eviction; MALI: Ex-general Appears To Win Election.

2002-05-17.title: In Pakistan, Rape Victims Are the 'Criminals'
2002-05-17.abstract: Case of Zafran Bibi, Pakistani rape victim, raises outcry, even in Pakistan, because of her sentence of death by stoning, punishment called for by Koranic law but never carried out there before; human rights workers say that in Pakistan, as many as half the women who report rape are charged with adultery; say even if Zafran's case returns to more typical course, she will spend 10 to 15 years in prison as result of her rape; note that men can easily deflect accusation of rape by claiming victim consented; say abuse of women is endemic in Pakistan; photo; map (M)

2002-05-17.title: Party Organ In Cuba Prints Speech By Carter
2002-05-17.abstract: Newspaper of Cuba's Communist Party, in highly unusual gesture of openness, publishes uncensored text of former Pres Jimmy Carter's speech in which he bluntly criticized Cuba's government for denying basic freedoms and endorsed referendum to improve rights; referendum campaign itself has been subjected to news blackout in state-run media, even after supporters garnered more than 10,000 signatures on petitions; newspaper Granma devotes five pages to Carter's speech and to his detailed responses to audience members who denounced referendum campaign, known as Project Varela, as foreign creation; Granma does not act on Carter's suggestion that it also print demands of project: for electoral law reform, amnesty for political prisoners, freedom of expression and right to own small businesses (M)

2002-05-17.title: World Briefing | Europe: Germany: Making Posthumous Amends
2002-05-17.abstract: Berlin Mayor Klaus Wowereit presents citizenship certificate for Marlene Dietrich to her grandson Peter Riva; Dietrich left Germany in 1930 and became United States citizen, criticizing Nazis; she had hostile reception in Berlin in 1960 and vowed not to return; photo (S)

2002-05-18.title: Still Raw From Recent Attack, India Is Girding Itself for War
2002-05-18.abstract: Indian officials say they are considering military action against Pakistan, which they blame for terrorist attacks of May 14 that killed 32 people; also plan punitive diplomatic steps; some in government voice bitter disappointment that US has been unable to persuade Pakistan, ally against terrorism in Afghanistan, to stop sponsoring terrorism against India; photo of demonstration by supporters of Hindu nationlist Bharatiya Janata Party outside Pakistani Embassy in New Delhi (M)

2002-05-18.title: His Mood Seems Mellow; His Cartoons Are Not
2002-05-18.abstract: Profile of Rafael Barajas, dean of Mexico's large and vigorous corps of political cartoonists; his cartoons, which appear in leftist La Jornada, frequently take aim at Pres Vicente Vox, whom he says is totally incable of living up to changes that he promised; photo (M)

2002-05-18.title: Carter Urges Bush to Reject Plan to Aid Cuba Dissidents
2002-05-18.abstract: Former Pres Jimmy Carter urges Pres Bush not to provide assistance for dissidents, human rights advocates and independent journalists in Cuba; says dissidents he met with are deeply concerned that such direct help from US would allow Pres Fidel Castro to discredit their efforts; adviser to Carter says many dissidents also favor lifting trade embargo, which Bush wants to tighten (M)

2002-05-18.title: World Briefing | Americas: Mexico: Fox Promises Water For U.S.
2002-05-18.abstract: Mexico will pay debt of billions of gallons of water it owes US under 1944 treaty; much of water has been diverted before reaching Rio Grande by officials in Chihuahua (S)

2002-05-18.title: Chancellor's Hair Color Is Now Ward of the Court
2002-05-18.abstract: German court upholds injunction that effectively prohibits news organizations from even hinting that Chancellor Gerhard Schroder colors his hair; Schroder's efforts to suppress such reports is mercilessly lampooned in ads run by cosmetics company for new hair strengthener (S)

2002-05-19.title: Pentagon's Worry: Iraqi Chemical Arms
2002-05-19.abstract: Military planners in Pentagon preparing for possible invasion of Iraq say most complicated problem they face is chance that Pres Saddam Hussein might use chemcial or biological weapons against American forces of their allies; this has prompted discussion of host of pre-emptive options for destroying his stockpiles or limiting his ability to use them; Iraq possessed chemical and probably biological weapons during Persian Gulf war of 1991; it did not use them, possibly because of American threats of retaliation; now that Pres Bush has place himself behind effort to change governments in Iraq, military experts say they must assume that Hussein would use every weapon in his arsenal (M)

2002-05-19.title: Rightist's Hard Line Appeals to War-Weary Colombians
2002-05-19.abstract: Alvaro Uribe, ultraconservative candidate for president in Colombia, has made promise to crack down on leftist rebels; he holds commanding lead in polls and is expected to defeat his nearest rival, Horacio Serpa, when election is held next week; what is uncertain is what effects his policies would have on his country and what they would mean for United States, which has provided nearly $2 billion in mostly military aid since 2000; Uribe says he will break from policies of current president, Andres Pastrana, whose peace talks with rebels collapsed; says he will become 'first soldier of Colombia' in campaign against rebels, promising to double size of army's combat force and police and to organize one million villagers to serve as informants; conflict-weary voters seem to be overlooking fact that Uribe is independent candidate representing far right in centrist country; photo; map (M)

2002-05-19.title: To the Drums of War, India Expels Pakistan Ambassador
2002-05-19.abstract: India seems to be taking steps needed to go to war with Pakistan; expels Pakistani Amb Ashraf Jehangir Qazi to protest attack by three heavily armed gunmen that it accuses Pakistan of sponsoring; gunmen killed 32 people, including wives and children of Indian Army soldiers; Indian Army, Navy and Air Force chiefs brief Prime Min Atal Behari Vajpayee on military options for countering what India regards as Pakistan-sponsored terrorism in Jammu and Kashmir, territory claimed by both countries; Indian and Pakistani troops bombard each other with heavy mortar and artillery fire; United States State Dept is expected to work intensely to persuade Pakistan to be more effective in shutting down militant Islamic groups based in Pakistan-held Kashmir (M)

2002-05-19.title: In Europe, Going Global Means, Alas, English
2002-05-19.abstract: Many European banks and corporations are making English official corporate language as way to leverage international links and enable far-flung affiliates to communicate with headquarters and among themselves; behind choice lies reality that unsettles some Europeans: use of English is mainly determined by unchallenged dominance of United States in industry, commerce and finance; photos (M)

2002-05-19.title: For Militant, No Glorified End, but Death in the Dust
2002-05-19.abstract: Riaz Basra, Pakistan's most feared Islamic militant, is brought down by simple villager armed with .22-caliber rifle and few neighbors with old pump-action rifles and handguns; Basra, head of dreaded Sunni extremist group Lashkar-i-Jangvi, led life brimming with murderous action along with legend of miraculous escapes; he met his end in village of Dakota, Pakistan, in shootout with Fida Hussain Ghalvi, who has kept watch every night since Basra killed his brother and other villagers in 1997; groups like Basra's have usually reserved their wrath for fellow Muslims, and have not become well known in West; photos; map (M)

2002-05-20.title: Bush Faces Pressure From Congress to Alter Cuba Policy
2002-05-20.abstract: Growing number of lawmakers from both parties are visiting Cuba on their own and demanding that US open it up to tourism and trade, even as Pres Bush heads to Miami to lay out tougher restrictions on travel to Cuba, and to lend support for government opponents there; will condition any easing of restrictions on adoption of democratic reforms, such as freeing all political prisoners and giving opposition political parties freedom to organize and speak; is committed personally and politically to hard-line policy toward Cuba; pivotal role played by Cuban-Americans in his victory in 2000 presidential election noted (M)

2002-05-20.title: Afghans Say 5 Killed in a U.S. Raid Were Farmers
2002-05-20.abstract: Afghans say five people killed in May 12 raid by US Special Forces on village of Char Chine in Oruzgan province were farmers; American military spokesman says that troops raided compound suspected of being sanctuary for senior members of Taliban and Al Qaeda and that five killed opened fire first; villagers describe raid; map; photos (M)

2002-05-20.title: Developers Threaten to Gobble Up a Famous Hong Kong Market
2002-05-20.abstract: Many Hong Kong merchants and longtime residents are appalled by plans by developers to demolish Stanley Market, island's most famous Chinese street market, to build nine-story hotel and air conditioned shopping mall; while other street markets have already been bulldozed and replaced by malls with little protest, Stanley's merchants and Hong Kong's small community of historic preservationists seem determined to fight latest scheme (M)

2002-05-20.title: U.S. Soldier Killed in a Firefight in Afghanistan
2002-05-20.abstract: American Special Forces soldier is killed in Afghanistan after unit came under attack while on patrol in eastern mountains where coalition forces have been operating; is identified as Sgt Gene Arden Vance Jr of Morgantown, W Va; photo (S)

2002-05-20.title: Chaos Prompts Exodus, Changing a Capital's Face
2002-05-20.abstract: The exodus of Venezuelans, desparate over deepening political divisions, is the latest chapter in a reversal for a country that was once a magnet for immigrants.

2002-05-21.title: Afghan Warlord Muddles Interim Government's Plans
2002-05-21.abstract: Afghan warlord Padsha Khan Zadran, still controls large parts of Khost and Pakita Province despite efforts of interim government of Hamid Karzai to oust him; situation has become most glaring example of government's inability to control events outside capital of Kabul; photo (M)

2002-05-21.title: Charmed by Their Leader, the Irish Ask Him to Stay
2002-05-21.abstract: Irish voters elect Prime Min Bertie Ahern for second term, at time when disaffected European electorates are turning against incumbent governments; dimensions of victory are huge, with his Fianna Fail party sending traditional foe, Fine Gael, to humiliating defeat and coming within several seats of winning majority in 166-seat Parliament; his presidential-style campaign, in which he traveled 3,000 miles and canvassed all 42 districts, discussed; brief biographical profile; photo (M)

2002-05-21.title: U.S. Gives More Details Of Clash Where G.I. Died
2002-05-21.abstract: US military officials say Sgt Gene Arden Vance Jr, Army Special Forces soldier killed on May 19 in Afghanistan on reconnaissance mission with allied Afghan troops near Pakistan border, died in brief battle in which an Afghan soldier was wounded and one adversary was believed to have been killed; coalition forces press search for attackers (S)

2002-05-21.title: Sharon Ejects Party From Israeli Cabinet
2002-05-21.abstract: Prime Minister Ariel Sharon of Israel fired ministers from the rigorously Orthodox Shas Party from his cabinet after they voted against an emergency economic package in Parliament.

2002-05-21.title: Tensions Mount Over Kashmir as Separatist Leader Is Killed
2002-05-21.abstract: Abdul Ghani Lone, a moderate Kashmiri separatist leader, was assassinated today, a killing that India and Pakistan immediately charged each other with.

2002-05-22.title: Accra Journal; No Mosh Pit, This, but Some Enthusiasm for Aid
2002-05-22.abstract: Rock star Bono and and Treasury Sec Paul H O'Neill visit Accra, Ghana, to start off their two-week fact-finding tour of Africa; pairing of Bono and O'Neill illustrates expanding ideological consensus on need to address poverty, disease, famine and conflict in Africa--as well as remaining gulf between left and right about how best to do it; Bono wants to convince Bush administration that aid to Africa is both moral imperative and good investment of taxpayers' money; O'Neill makes clear that US will be skeptical about providing aid to countries that cannot show how they are improving standards of living; photo of Bono and O'Neill with Pres John A Kufuor; map (M)

2002-05-22.title: American Planes Foil an Attack on an Airfield in Afghanistan
2002-05-22.abstract: American warplanes bomb group of suspected Qaeda and Taliban fighters as they are setting up mortars within striking distance of airfield at Khost, pivotal hub for allied troops in Afghanistan; Canada announces that it will withdraw its 850 troops in Afghanistan this summer (S)

2002-05-22.title: Sharon's Domestic Battle: A Gamble
2002-05-22.abstract: News analysis: Israeli Prime Min Ariel Sharon's latest bold gamble of kicking ministers of Shas, religious Sephardic party, out of his government was impressive display of brinkmanship, given their importance to his coalition; Sharon acted after Shas voted against his proposed emergency economic package, intended to address budget deficit caused in large measure by military offensive he recently ordered in West Bank; dismissal is likely to bolster his already soaring popularity among Isralies, but also threatens to leave his government with barely enough certain votes in Parliament to stave off new elections; if Sharon becomes embroiled in fight for political survival, international efforts to press for peace would probably be stymied for months; photo (M)

2002-05-22.title: World Briefing | Asia: South Korea: Leader Urges Calm For World Cup
2002-05-22.abstract: Pres Kim Dae Jung calls for moratorium on strikes and political infighting in South Korea through end of World Cup soccer finals on June 30; photo (S)

2002-05-22.title: In Bahrain, Women Run, Women Vote, Women Lose
2002-05-22.abstract: Bahrain holds municipal elections in which women participate for first time in nation's history as both candidates and voters, but all 31 women running in field of more than 300 candidates lose; all 50 seats on five councils are won by candidates affilated with Islamic organizatons; voting for a man was turned into religious duty in many neighborhoods; female candidates had difficulty meeting voters in country still largely segregated by sex; photos; map (M)

2002-05-23.title: World Briefing | Asia: Kyrgyzstan: Prime Minister Quits
2002-05-23.abstract: Kyrgyzstan Prime Min Kurmanbek Bakiyev and cabinet resign amid growing unrest since death of five demonstrators in clashes with police in March (S)

2002-05-23.title: British Leave Pakistan
2002-05-23.abstract: Britain urges its citizens to leave Pakistan, orders departure of more than 150 personnel from its embassy and closes its consulates; actions are in response to rising threats against Westerners in Pakistan since May 8, when car bomb exploded outside hotel in Karachi, killing 11 French citizens and 3 Pakistanis

2002-05-23.title: Indian Leader's Threat of War Rattles Pakistan and the U.S.
2002-05-23.abstract: Prime Min Atal Behari Vajpayee tells Indian soldiers along tense frontier in Kashmir to prepare for 'decisive battle' against terrorism; uses words powerful enough to rally his troops, threaten Pakistan and scare much of world; India has grim choice: make good on pledge to retaliate forcefully against militant groups based in Pakistani territory, or appear at home and abroad as nation that issues meaningless threats; prevailing expectation is that India will strike punitive blow against militants' training camps in Pakistan-controlled area of disputed Jammu and Kashmir State; overarching fear is that escalating confrontation might unleash nuclear combat; two nations have been exchanging artillery fire across frontier for last five days; Pakistan responds to Vajpayee's latest statement saying it would use 'full force' if attacked; worried diplomats from United States and elsewhere schedule visits to New Delhi and Islamabad; photo; map (M)

2002-05-23.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Iran: U.S. Terror Charge Denied
2002-05-23.abstract: Iran dismisses US State Department report charging Iran is 'most active state sponsor of terrorism' (S)

2002-05-23.title: As Bush Heads for Russia, Hopes for an Investment Plan Fade
2002-05-23.abstract: Many Russians are disappointed at indications that Pres Bush will not be discussing economic proposals during his Kremlin meeting with Pres Vladimir V Putin; Russian government announces series of agreements and accords two presidents will sign, but there is no mention of investments, economic incentives or other policy changes that would help stimulate Russian economy (M)

2002-05-24.title: Israelis Consider New Limits on West Bank Palestinians
2002-05-24.abstract: Israeli Army is stringing barbed wire around Ramallah on West Bank in hope of preventing Palestinian attacks; international aid workers fear this is part of sweeping new restrictions that will further squeeze Palestinians' already crippled economy and perhaps stoke more violence; aid officials say Israeli Army has outlined plan to create eight zones in West Bank, around each of its major cities; say Palestinians needing to travel would apply for special permit to enter or leave zone, and permits, to be renewed monthly, would be valid only during day; if put into effect plan would amount to substantial tightening of existing Israeli policy; for international aid agencies that move food, medicine or construction material, plan would mean tremendous increase in workers, costs and time spent in transport; photo; map (M)

2002-05-24.title: Pakistan Prepares to Shift Troops From Afghan Border to Kashmir
2002-05-24.abstract: Pakistan prepares to shift troops from border with Afghanistan, where they are hunting for Qaeda and Taliban fighters, to front in Kashmir, where tensions with India are quickly rising; government announcement underscores competing interests between ferreting out terrorist remnants while securing region against possibility of full-blown war between nuclear-armed rivals; India and Pakistan have million troops already massed along their 1,800-mile border; two armies are shelling each other with mounting intensity across dividing line in Kashmir; Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld expresses concern, noting that because of conflict, Pakistan has placed forces along Indian borders that could be used to help American soldiers along Afghan border (M)

2002-05-24.title: World Briefing | Europe: France: Still Defiant In Beef Dispute
2002-05-24.abstract: France's new agriculture minister, Herve Gaymard, says he will defy European Court of Justice order to lift ban on British beef; ban was imposed in 1996 because of fear of mad cow disease; Britian holds France should be fined (S)

2002-05-24.title: U.N. Extends Tenure Of Afghan Forces
2002-05-24.abstract: Security Council extends tenure of international troops in Afghanistan for another six months but choses not to expand their ranks, as Afghanistan's interim government and some senior UN officials had urged (S)

2002-05-24.title: U.S. and Russia Sign Nuclear Weapons Reduction Treaty
2002-05-24.abstract: President Bush and President Vladimir V. Putin signed a treaty today reducing their countries' warheads by two thirds.

2002-05-25.title: World Briefing: Asia
2002-05-25.abstract: Asia South KOREA: Political Truce For Soccer; NEPAL: Political Crisis.

2002-05-25.title: At Tense Time, Pakistan Starts To Test Missiles
2002-05-25.abstract: Pakistan risks setting off new crisis with India by beginning series of missile tests; Pakistani government denies any provocative intent, and Indian Foreign Ministry spokeswoman dismisses 'missile antics' that she says are targeted at domestic audience in Pakistan; United States says it is 'disappointed' by Pakistan's decision to test missiles, coming day after Pakistan said it would move troops toward India; State Dept issues warning to Americans to defer travel to India and Pakistan because of risk of war; even with Pakistani decision, tensions appear to have lessened; Indian government officials say they will give Pakistan more time, perhaps few weeks, to demonstrate its commitment to stopping infiltration of armed militants into India's side of Kashmir before undertaking any punitive military strikes; photo; map (M)

2002-05-25.title: Learning curves : Helping children break the language barrier
2002-05-25.abstract: BERLIN—  One recent bedtime, five-year-old Alec Kubekov read an English book to his mother, a selection from the "Biff and Chip" series. As his father walked in to say goodnight, Alec began translating the book into Russian. "It was a good translation," said his mother. "He was translating for sense, not word for word."

2002-05-25.title: Web Sites Remove Video of Pearl's Death
2002-05-25.abstract: Federal Bureau of Investigation asks at least two Internet sites to remove video showing questioning and beheading of Daniel Pearl, Wall Street Journal reporter who was kidnapped and murdered in Pakistan; hosts of two sites are said to comply with request, which FBI spokeswoman says was made out of consideration for Pearl's family (S)

2002-05-25.title: U.S. Review of a Deadly Afghanistan Battle Finds Lapses
2002-05-25.abstract: American officials say military review of battle in eastern Afghanistan in early March that left seven American soldiers dead found intelligence lapses, radio glitches and miscommunication between commanders and commandos; clash came on third day of three-week-long campaign in Shah-i-Kot Valley, and stemmed from attempt to rescue Navy Seal who had fallen out of helicopter; Gen Tommy R Franks, head of allied forces in Afghanistan, defends operation, and attributes any failings to clever fighters from Al Qaeda, unforgiving terrain and 'fog and friction' of war; says no disciplinary action or command changes are being considered as result of that battle; senior Pentagon military officer who conducted review of battle says he sent report on how to improve 'tactics, techniques and procedures' used by military forces involved in fight (M)

2002-05-26.title: 3 North Koreans in Beijing Defect to Seoul
2002-05-26.abstract: South Korean Embassy in Beijing says that three North Koreans sought refuge there, and South Korean government demands that Chinese authorities treat case in 'humanitarian manner' (S)

2002-05-26.title: Swiss Overrule Lavish Home In a Clash Over Rural Values
2002-05-26.abstract: Environmentalists in Switzerland prevail in their campaign to prevent auto racing champion Michael Schumacher from building lavish home in area zoned for agriculture; while most Swiss live in cities, they are greatly attached to concept of rural life, and farmers are heavily subsidized to keep them on the land; land use is strictly regulated (M)

2002-05-26.title: Seeking Trade, Africans Find Western Barriers
2002-05-26.abstract: African leaders, along with some of poverty-fighting advocacy groups that for years have viewed globalization with suspicion if not downright hostility, are increasingly embracing international trade as most powerful tool available for creating jobs, steady incomes, hope and political stability; after concentrating for years on winning relief from crippling debt burdens and lobbying for increases in foreign aid payments, African governments are instead starting to demand reduced tariffs and other changes in trade policies from United States and other rich nations to increase sales of local products; photo of Treasury Sec Paul O'Neill, who is traveling through Africa with Bono, musician (M)

2002-05-26.title: Colombia Voters Are Angry, And Rebels May Pay Price
2002-05-26.abstract: Estimated 12 million Colombians are set to vote for new president, and many, angry about rebel violence, plan to vote for hard-line conservative candidate, Alvara Uribe Velez, who promises to double size of security forces to fight rebels; his main opponent is Horacio Serpa, who is far behind in polls; many Colombians are fed up with regime of Pres Andres Pastrana, who came into office four years ago pledging to negotiate with leftist rebels; photos (M)

2002-05-26.title: Germany May Extradite Islamic Militant to Turkey
2002-05-26.abstract: German officials negotiate with Turkish government to extradite Metin Kaplan, Turk who is serving four-year sentence in Germany for operating terrorist organization and calling for murder of rival; Kaplan is leader of banned terror group known as Caliphate; is wanted in Turkey on charges of involvement in failed 1998 plot to fly airplane filled with explosives into mausoleum of Kemal Ataturk, founder of Turkish republic, in Ankara; Kaplan's organization, which once had 12,000 members in Germany, advocates killing of non-Muslims (M)

2002-05-27.title: Hard-Liner Elected in Colombia With a Mandate to Crush Rebels
2002-05-27.abstract: Alvaro Uribe Velez, hardliner, is elected president of Colombia with 53 percent of vote, or more than 20 percentage points ahead of closest rival Horacio Serpa, Liberal Party populist; wins majority needed to avoid runoff; he pledges uncompromising 'hard hand' against insurgents; quickly receives backing of US and congratuations from Amb Anne Patterson; photo (M)

2002-05-27.title: 12 Million Could Die at Once in an India-Pakistan Nuclear War
2002-05-27.abstract: American intelligence assessmentwarns that full-scale nuclear exchange between India and Pakistan could kill up to 12 million people immediately and injury up to 7 million; even 'more limited' nuclear war would have cataclysmic results, overwhelming hospitals across Asia and requiring vast foreign assistance, particularly from US, to battle radioactive contamination, famine and disease (M)

2002-05-27.title: North Koreans defect to embassy in China
2002-05-27.abstract: SEOUL:—  Three North Koreans have sought refuge in the South Korean Embassy in Beijing and the South Korean government has demanded that Chinese authorities treat the case "in a humanitarian manner."

2002-05-27.title: As Shells Fall on Kashmiri Border Village, Residents Fear Outbreak of War
2002-05-27.abstract: Shells from Pakistani positions over mountain fall on Kashmiri border village of Uri, as threat of India-Pakistan war looms over valley; photo; map (M)

2002-05-27.title: Rome Journal; A Faux Treaty Room, With Missiles
2002-05-27.abstract: Rome Journal on elaborate setting and security for meeting of NATO and Russian leaders at Practica di Mare military base outside Italian capital; stage sets devised by Prime Min Silvio Berlusconi described; photo (M)

2002-05-28.title: Pakistan Vows Its 'Full Might' If a War Comes
2002-05-28.abstract: Pres Pervez Musharraf says in nationwide address that Pakistan does not want conflict with India but will 'respond with full might' if war breaks out; urges India to open dialogue and to move toward normalization of relations; lays full blame for standoff on India; repeats he will not allow Pakistan to be used as staging ground for terror attacks, India's chief complaint against him; denies Islamic militants are crossing into India's side of Kashmir to carry out raids; pledges support for those resisting Indian rule in mostly Muslim region; Indian officials react with anger to address, finding little by way of conciliation; maps; photo (M)

2002-05-28.title: Bush's Memorial Words: 'Sacrifice of the Young'
2002-05-28.abstract: Excerpts from Pres Bush's Memorial Day speech at American Cemetery in Normandy, France (M)

2002-05-28.title: World Briefing | Africa: Lesotho: Government Ahead In Vote
2002-05-28.abstract: Early results from parliamentary election in Losotho show governing party taking strong lead (S)

2002-05-28.title: World Briefing | Asia: China: Muslims In Afghanistan
2002-05-28.abstract: Chinese official reports that 300 Uighur Muslims from China's western province of Xinjiang were captured in Afghanistan fighting with Taliban forces and that hundreds more Chinese Muslim separatists remain hidden in Afghanistan and Pakistan (S)

2002-05-28.title: Colombian President-Elect Softens Tone on Rebels
2002-05-28.abstract: Colombia's Pres-elect Alvaro Uribe Velez, who won presidency promising to crack down on leftist rebels, strikes conciliatory tone by holding out possibility of negotiating with guerrilla commanders under UN mediation; hopes to set up meeting with Sec Gen Kofi Annan; says negotiations with right-wing paramilitary group, bitter enemy of rebels, are possible--proposition predecessors declined to consider because of widespread rights abuses perpetuated by organization; photos (M)

2002-05-29.title: NATO Formally Welcomes Russia as a Partner
2002-05-29.abstract: NATO formally welcomes Russia as partner but not full-fledged member in organization created 53 years ago to contain Soviet power and expansion; meeting of leaders of 19 member nations in Rome includes Pres Bush and Pres Vladimir Putin; Bush says agreement joins former foes as partners, overcoming 50 years of division and decade of uncertainty; similar sentiments are echoed by Putin, who nevertheless describes agreement as only a beginning, noting that relations between NATO and Russia have historically been far from straightforward; new agreement replaces 1997 accord negotiated during Clinton administration that allowed Russia to participate in discussions only after alliance's members reached agreement on common position; gives Russia for first time immediate role in NATO discussions but not veto power over decisions or vote on expansion of membership; excludes Russia from collective defense pact in which all nation's pledge to come to each other's defense if necessary; photos (M)

2002-05-29.title: Arab Rakes Israeli Yeshiva With Gunfire; 3 Students Die
2002-05-29.abstract: Palestinian gunman fires on Israeli settlement near Nablus, killing three young yeshiva students before being shot and killed; Israel dies in road ambush near Ramallah; attacks follow five suicide bombings in less than week; compound evidence that recent major Israeli military offensive did little to blunt Palestinian attacks; Israeli raids in Jenin, Hebron, Beit Jala and Qalqilya in West Bank leads to arrests of 14 Palestinians; Army remains in control of Bethlehem; map; photo (M)

2002-05-29.title: Pakistani Intelligence Officials See Qaeda Peril in Their Cities
2002-05-29.abstract: Senior Pakistani intelligence officials say recent terror attacks point to worrisome links between local extremists and fugitive Al Qaeda leaders who are not concentrated along Afghan border, as American officials contend, but have filtered across country into major cities; say imminent battle may be one for Pakistan itself (M)

2002-05-29.title: Train Wrecks and Other Headaches: A British Minister Quits
2002-05-29.abstract: Stephen Byers, Britain's transport secretary responsible for country's ailing rail and road systems, resigns after turbulent one-year tenure marred by crashes and service disruptions on trains, increased tie-ups on roads and high-profile scandals in his office (M)

2002-05-29.title: New Transport Secretary Named for Britain's Troubled System
2002-05-29.abstract: Prime Minister Tony Blair today named his work and pensions secretary to the job of running Britain's transportation system.

2002-05-30.title: World Briefing | Asia: Taiwan: 'Black Boxes' From Crash Located
2002-05-30.abstract: Officials in Taiwan say they pinpointed seafloor location of data and cockpit voice recorders from Hong Kong-bound China Airlines Boeing 747 that broke into four pieces in midflight on May 25, apparently killing all 225 people aboard (S)

2002-05-30.title: Canary Islands Journal; Squalid Dead End for Migrants' Hopes
2002-05-30.abstract: Canary Islands must contend with influx of African migrants who make hazardous journey on small wooden boats to island of Fuerteventura, hoping it will be their first step toward better life; all illegal immigrants except for pregnant women and small children are locked in terminal for full 40 days before being deported; aid workers complain about poor conditions for detainees; photos; map (M)

2002-05-30.title: Cryptic Tapes From 2000 Hinted at Air Attack in U.S.
2002-05-30.abstract: Italian and German investigators disclose fresh information suggesting that hints of attack involving aircraft and US were more widespread among European law enforcement agencies before Sept 11 than previously suspected; German investigators say communications among Al Qaeda cells has recently picked up; Italian officials note that conversations they intercepted were from summer of 2000, but say they demonstrate that Al Qaeda was organizing something (M)

2002-05-30.title: World Briefing | Asia: Philippines: U.S. Bounty For Militants' Arrest
2002-05-30.abstract: United States offers reward of up to $5 million for information leading to arrest of five top leaders of militant Philippine Muslim group Abu Sayyaf; group is holding two American missionaries hostage, Martin and Gracia Burnham; Deputy Defense Sec Paul D Wolfowitz to visit southern Philippines next week to check on progress of rescue operation (S)

2002-05-30.title: Mexico Misses Deadline for Water-Debt Plan
2002-05-30.abstract: Pres Vicente Fox's aides say he will not fulfill his pledge to Pres Bush to meet deadline for plan to pay Mexico's enormous water debt to United States; Mexico owes 456 billion gallons under 1944 treaty on dividing waters of Rio Grande; Fox's inability to deliver quickly on his personal pledge to Bush to solve waer crisis raises questions about his ability to govern (M)

2002-05-31.title: World Briefing: Americas
2002-05-31.abstract: PERU: Court To Seek Fujimori Extradition; Mexico CITY: Delay On Two-level Highways; MEXICO: All Cyanide Recovered; CUBA: Spy Suspect Arrested.

2002-05-31.title: Bush Presses Pakistan on Kashmir And Orders Rumsfeld to Region
2002-05-31.abstract: Pres Bush will send Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld to India and Pakistan in effort to defuse threat of nuclear war between longtime enemies; makes strongest demand yet that Pres Pervez Musharraf of Pakistan crack down on infiltration of terrorists into India, amid growing alarm that five-month-long military buildup has reached new point of crisis; Sec Colin L Powell is sending his deputy, Richard L Armitage, to region next week; Bush has been praising Musharraf for his pledge to clamp down on Islamic extremists who carry out attacks on Indian civilians along line of separation, and newly harsh tone appears aimed at assuaging Indian rage over incursions; Bush also warns remnants of Al Qaeda thought to be gathered in western Pakistan not to exploit India-Pakistan conflict to make trouble along Pakistan's western border (M)

2002-05-31.title: Son Is Born to Widow of Reporter Killed in Karachi
2002-05-31.abstract: Son is born to Mariane Pearl, widow of Wall Street Journal reporter Daniel Pearl who was kidnapped and killed by Islamic militants in Pakistan four months ago (S)

2002-05-31.title: Canadian Leader Attacked In a Loud Ethics Brouhaha
2002-05-31.abstract: Prime Min Jean Chretien freezes dozens of government contracts, reorganizes his cabinet and issues new rules on political donations and conflicts of interest; he is struggling to contain worst ethics controversy to face his Liberal Party in years, involving what opposition calls pattern of government ministers' handing out contracts to friends and companies who donate to party; Globe and Mail, Canada's most influential newspaper, calls for Chretien to resign; he has held office since 1993 and will probably survive crisis, but controversy has already intensified jostling among party politicians likely to use gathering of Liberals in Feb to position themselves as his successor (M)

2002-05-31.title: World Briefing: Europe
2002-05-31.abstract: Europe RUSSIA: Communist Leader Denounces Summit ; BRITAIN: Guilty Verdict In Foot-and-mouth Case.
